The data copied over to the Work Order form when creating a work order from a case cannot beconfigured or customized

questions.explanation: A key synchronization limitation is that if information changes on the Case form, it is NOT automatically updated on the associated Work Order form. This one-way synchronization means that changes made to case details don't propagate to the work order, potentially causing data inconsistencies that require manual updates.

questions.question: Which roles can propose a case as a Major Case candidate? (Choose three.)
questions.explanation: Three roles can propose cases as Major Case candidates: Customer service manager, Customer service agent, and Major issue manager. Proxy contacts and Customer case managers do not have this proposal capability, emphasizing that major case escalation is primarily handled by service management and major issue management roles.

questions.question: Which of the following is a required field in the Resolution Information tab in order to close a case?
questions.explanation: Resolution notes are a required field in the Resolution Information tab to close a case. This field ensures agents document the solution provided, maintaining audit trails and supporting knowledge management. Other fields like Cause, Closed, and Closed by are either optional or auto-populated.

questions.question: Access to a Knowledge base or Article can be restricted based on a customer's assets and the product models using which of the following?
questions.explanation: Knowledge Product Entitlements allow organizations to restrict knowledge base or article access based on customer assets and product models. This ensures customers only see information relevant to their installed products and assets, improving relevance and security. Data Policies, ACLs, and User Criteria are separate access control mechanisms.

questions.question: What benefits does scoping an application bring? (Choose three.)

options.text: CSM teams can move at their desired pace, independent of IT
Provides CSM teams autonomy and control
CSM application data and business logic is protected from changes by other applications
Account records can be changed only while in the CSM scope
Changes in different scopes can be addressed in a single update set

questions.explanation: Scoping provides three key benefits: CSM teams can move at their desired pace independent of IT departments, teams gain autonomy and control over their application, and CSM business logic is protected from unintended changes by other applications. Scope limitations only affect records within that scope, not across the platform.

questions.question: A customer service agent wants to escalate an account but is unable to use the Escalate Account related link on the Account form. What could be the reasons why the customer service agent is not able to use it? (Choose two.)

options.text: No escalation approval flow is configured
The parent account of the account to be escalated is not active
The customer service agent is not assigned with the escalation requester role
The account already has an open escalation record

questions.explanation: Two reasons would prevent account escalation: absence of a configured escalation approval flow, and the customer service agent lacking the escalation requester role assignment. Having an open escalation record doesn't necessarily block new escalations, and the parent account status doesn't prevent escalation functionality.

questions.question: After installing the Performance Analytics Content Pack for Customer Service, which job must be run to retrieve daily case data from previous months?
questions.explanation: The Customer Service Historic Data Collection job must be executed to retrieve case data from previous months before the content pack installation. This job backfills historical data to provide complete analytics dashboards. Daily Collection job only captures new data, while Initial and Case Collection jobs serve different purposes.

questions.question: When working with communication channels, what inbound email flows are available by default? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Two inbound email flows are available by default: 'Create case from email' (initiating new cases from incoming emails) and 'Update case using reply' (updating existing cases through email replies). These core flows enable email-based case management, while other options like creating cases for products or assets require additional configuration.

questions.question: What determines how an escalation request is processed?
questions.explanation: Escalation Template determines how an escalation request is processed. Templates define the workflow, approval chain, notification recipients, and processing steps for escalations. Templates allow organizations to standardize and customize escalation handling based on severity, escalation type, or other criteria.

questions.question: What allows the implementation of phases and tasks to meet performance goals, track progress and measure success?
questions.explanation: Continual Improvement Management enables organizations to implement structured phases and tasks aligned with performance goals, track progress systematically, and measure success through defined metrics. This framework supports ongoing service quality improvement. Performance Analytics provides reporting, SLA Tasks enforce SLAs, and Scheduled Reporting automates distribution.

questions.question: For security purposes, certain roles cannot be assigned to a group or individual at the same time. Which of the following two roles would be restricted?
questions.explanation: Roles 'snc_internal' and 'snc_external' are mutually exclusive for security reasons. An individual or group cannot hold both internal (snc_internal) and external (snc_external) user roles simultaneously, as this would create security and access control conflicts. This segregation ensures proper role-based access control enforcement.

options.text: Bring important information about individual records to an agent's attention

To ensure customers get the service they are entitled to receive

questions.explanation:
The correct answer is A. Special Handling Notes are designed to bring important information to an agent's attention for specific records.

Monitor alerts for operational services and configuration items that affect service health
Trace information provided m a case to the right product or service to which it relates
Close an upsell of related products and services not yet purchased by a customer

questions.explanation: The install base shows customer's product configurations and service details. Option A allows agents to see detailed configurations to determine needed actions. Option C enables tracing case information to related products/services.

questions.question: Which step in Advanced Work Assignment (AWA) would ensure the work was allocated to the appropriate agent?
questions.explanation: Advanced Work Assignment has multiple steps: Agent Experience, Assignment Rules, Work Item Queues, and Service Channels. Assignment Rules specifically determine how work is routed to appropriate agents based on predefined criteria.

questions.question: When working with case types, what is the lowest level in the case type hierarchy called?
questions.explanation: Case type hierarchy is structured with multiple levels. The lowest level in this hierarchy is called 'Leaf-level', which represents the terminal nodes that cannot have further subdivisions.

questions.question: Which roles are responsible for maintaining account team membership? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Account team membership is maintained by two key roles: System Administrator [admin] and Customer Service Manager [sn_customerservice_manager]. These roles have the permissions required to add, remove, and manage team members.

questions.question: What can a person assigned with the customer role access on the customer service portal by default? (Choose three.)
questions.explanation: Customers with the customer role can access Cases, Assets, and Sold Products by default on the customer service portal. These provide visibility into their cases, related assets, and products they have purchased.

questions.question: Which of the following are benefits of customer access management? (Choose two.)

options.text: It increases security by automatically granting access to cases based on access to sold product.
It defaults the responsibility for access management to the customer.
It defaults the responsibility for access management to the customer service agent.
It improves the customer experience by enabling related parties to track and collaborate on cases.

questions.explanation: Customer access management provides two key benefits: (1) It increases security by automatically granting access to cases based on access to sold products, ensuring proper authorization; (2) It improves customer experience by enabling related parties to track and collaborate on cases, promoting transparency and teamwork.

questions.question: Which ServiceNow products can be integrated out-of-the-box with CSM? (Choose three.)
questions.explanation: CSM has out-of-the-box integrations with ITOM Event Management, Strategic Portfolio Management, and Financial Management. These integrations allow for seamless data flow and operational alignment across these ServiceNow modules.

questions.question: When activating the Customer Service Management Demo Data plugin, which case type is available besides product case?
questions.explanation: When activating the CSM Demo Data plugin, it provides sample data including an 'Order' case type in addition to the standard 'Product' case type. This allows users to practice with different case types during their learning phase.

questions.question: What are the different resource matching methods on the Matching Rule form? (Choose four.)
questions.explanation: The Matching Rule form supports four resource matching methods: Simple (basic criteria), Advanced (complex logic), Selection Criteria (defined conditions), and Scripted (custom scripts). These methods provide flexible ways to match work items with appropriate agents.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Guided Decisions capability?

options.text: Provide agents with an escalation guide
Dynamically assist agents through account management
Dynamically help agents to resolve complex cases
Provide agents with a knowledge g uide

questions.explanation: Guided Decisions dynamically assists agents in resolving complex cases by providing step-by-step guidance and decision trees. This capability helps agents navigate through complex case scenarios and improves case resolution outcomes.

questions.question: Which feature enables employees to request support for themselves and for external customers?
questions.explanation: Contributor Users is the feature that enables both internal employees and external customers to request support. This dual-mode functionality allows organizations to manage both internal IT support requests and external customer support requests through the same system.

questions.question: What functionality is required to automatically close resolved cases if customers do not respond within a specified time?

options.text: Auto Close Resolved Cases Business Rule
Auto Close Resolved Cases Scheduled Job
Auto Close Resolved Cases Flow Designer Flow

questions.explanation: Auto Close Resolved Cases Flow Designer Flow is the required functionality for automatically closing resolved cases when customers don't respond within a specified timeframe. Flow Designer provides the automation engine to implement this time-based closure logic.

questions.question: Advanced Work Assignment (AWA) automatically routes and assigns work items to agents based on which of the following rules? (Choose three.)
questions.explanation: AWA uses three key rule types for routing and assignment: Skills (matching agent capabilities with case requirements), Schedule (considering agent availability), and Workload (balancing work distribution). These rules work together to ensure optimal work distribution.

questions.question: Which feature provides easy-to-configure integrations between the Now Platform and third-party Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) tools or platforms?
questions.explanation: Integration Hub is the ServiceNow feature that provides pre-built, easy-to-configure integrations with third-party CRM and ERP systems. It enables organizations to quickly connect their CSM platform with external business applications.

questions.question: Which table does the Account (customer_account) table extend?
questions.explanation: The Account (customer_account) table extends the Company (core_company) table. This inheritance relationship means Account inherits all fields and functionality from the Company table while adding CSM-specific extensions.

questions.question: Which combination of roles is restricted for security purposes?
questions.explanation: The combination of 'snc_internal' and 'sn_customerservice.partner_admin' roles is restricted for security purposes. This restriction prevents users from being both internal system users and partner administrators simultaneously, which could create security conflicts and data access issues.

questions.question: What must a system administrator configure to define a different approval workflow for an escalation request?
questions.explanation: System administrators must configure an Escalation Template to define different approval workflows for escalation requests. Templates allow customization of the escalation process including approval chains and notification rules.

questions.question: Which entity represents specific versions of products being sold to and supported for customers?
questions.explanation: Product Models represent specific versions of products being sold to and supported for customers. Product Models track different versions, configurations, and variants of products in the install base, enabling detailed version management.

questions.question: After an assignment group is chosen, which mandatory step in Advanced Work Assignment (AWA) ensures the work is allocated to the appropriate agent?
questions.explanation: After selecting an assignment group, defining Assignment Rules is the mandatory step that ensures work is allocated to the appropriate agent. Assignment Rules specify the matching criteria and logic that direct work to qualified agents.

questions.question: Which decisions does Predictive Intelligence support? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Predictive Intelligence supports Case Prioritization and Case Categorization decisions. These AI-powered capabilities analyze case data to automatically recommend appropriate priority levels and categories, improving case handling efficiency.

questions.question: What is the term for a formal agreement between a service provider and a customer thatprovides the customer with an expectation of service within a known timescale?
questions.explanation: 服务级别协议（Service Level Agreement, SLA）是服务提供商与客户之间的正式协议，用于明确定义和保证服务的质量水平。该协议详细说明了客户期望接收的服务标准、响应时间、可用性、故障修复时间等具体指标，并在明确的时间范围内对这些承诺负责。SLA是现代IT服务管理中的核心概念，它建立了服务提供商和客户之间的期望管理机制，确保双方对服务质量有共同的理解。通过SLA，组织可以衡量服务表现、管理客户期望，并为未能达到的服务水平提供补偿机制。这使SLA成为服务合同中最重要的约定条款之一。

questions.question: Which Service-Aware Install Base entity defines configuration items accessible tocustomers?
questions.explanation: 安装基础项目（Install Base Item）是ServiceNow Service-Aware Install Base中的关键实体，用于定义和管理客户可以访问和使用的配置项。这些配置项代表了客户已购买和部署的产品及服务。Install Base Item允许组织将产品与客户相关联，并追踪客户拥有的特定产品实例及其配置。通过Service-Aware Install Base，企业能够基于客户已有的配置项提供更加个性化和针对性的服务和支持。Model用于定义产品类型，Sold Product表示销售的产品，而Asset代表更广泛的资产概念。Install Base Item则特别强调了从客户角度的可访问配置项，是客户关系管理中的核心构件。
